content.problemStatement:
Chef started watching a movie that runs for a total of XXX minutes.

Chef has decided to watch the first YYY minutes of the movie at **twice** the usual speed as he was warned by his friends that the movie gets interesting only after the first YYY minutes.

How long will Chef spend watching the movie in **total**?

**Note:** It is guaranteed that YYY is **even**.


Input Format

The first line contains two space separated integers X,YX, YX,Y - as per the problem statement.


Output Format

Print in a single line, an integer denoting the total number of minutes that Chef spends in watching the movie.
content.inputFormat: The input consists of one or more test cases. Each test case is on a separate line containing two space‑separated integers X and Y, where X is the total length of the movie in minutes and Y (even) is the number of initial minutes watched at double speed. The file ends with EOF.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the total number of minutes Chef actually spends watching the movie.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First 20 minutes are watched at double speed, taking 20/2 = 10 minutes. The remaining 80 minutes are watched normally, taking 80 minutes. Total = 10 + 80 = 90.
First 24 minutes need 24/2 = 12 minutes. Remaining 26 minutes need 26 minutes. Total = 12 + 26 = 38.
All 10 minutes are watched at double speed, so time spent = 10/2 = 5 minutes.

solution.approach: For the first Y minutes Chef watches at twice the speed, therefore the time spent on that part is Y/2 minutes. The remaining X‑Y minutes are watched at normal speed, taking X‑Y minutes. Total time = Y/2 + (X‑Y) = X - Y/2. Since Y is guaranteed even, Y/2 is an integer. For each test case we just compute X - Y/2 and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) where T is the number of test cases (constant work per case).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
